behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the handoff between multiple local modules: three source clients, aggregation/orchestration, and the summary-input builder. A unit test of one function can miss wiring/field-mapping omissions across modules.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline with test doubles for the three source adapters returning distinct sentinel values. Execute real orchestration and summary-input construction, then assert the payload sent to the summary generator contains all three sentinels in the expected sections/fields.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module fault handling: source failure propagation, orchestrator resilience, and report rendering fallback text. No real OS/network is strictly required if one adapter is forced to fail through the module interface.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collector/orchestrator/report-renderer modules. Configure one source adapter to raise the same exception type used by production code paths. Assert run completion, presence of `data unavailable` in that section, and successful output generation for remaining sections.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model output quality and non-deterministic generation behavior, not just deterministic wiring. Mocking or stubbing generation would hide hallucination/omission failures.
behaviors.test_description: Using fixed structured input data with known key values, call the real summary model and score output against a rubric: required key metrics/entities must be present and numerically consistent. Run multiple trials and fail if representation accuracy drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: This requires real external boundaries (HTTP/API calls and actual email transport/inbox delivery). Lower-level tests can pass while real delivery fails due to transport/auth/config/runtime issues.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled job in an environment with reachable test instances of data sources and a real mail path to a test inbox. Verify end-to-end completion and assert the received email in the inbox has expected subject, recipients, and report body structure.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: The real failure mode includes transport unreachability plus persistence/retry across runs. Pure mocks can miss real exception semantics and scheduling/run-boundary behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run 1: execute job with mail service actually unreachable (e.g., connection refused) and verify report is written to configured local fallback path. Run 2 (next scheduled invocation): restore mail service, execute again, and verify pending saved report is retried and delivered, with retry state cleared.
